name: Chartered Capital Advisers, Inc
description_short: Chartered Capital Advisers provides merger & acquisition, business valuation, and corporate financial advisory services to corporate clients, investors, and financial institutions since 1988.
description_long:
Chartered Capital Advisers, Inc. was established in 1988 with its New York City office and has served over 600 clients across various sizes and industries worldwide. The firm specializes in high-level corporate financial advisory services rather than traditional business lending, positioning itself as a boutique advisory practice rather than a loan originator. The company's focus is on helping clients navigate complex financial transactions, valuations, and strategic decisions.

The company offers a comprehensive suite of advisory services including business valuations, fairness opinions, merger and acquisition support, equity and debt securities pricing, intangible asset valuation, goodwill impairment analyses, acquisition due diligence, negotiation assistance, expert testimony, financial projections, and market analysis. Through its affiliate R. G. Quintero & Co., the firm also provides turnaround and workout services, financial restructuring, corporate bankruptcy guidance, and financial training programs including CFA review courses.

Chartered Capital Advisers distinguishes itself through its team composition—all professionals have held senior positions at Big Four accounting firms, investment banks, commercial banks, valuation firms, or in private industry leadership roles. This emphasis on highly credentialed, experienced professionals sets them apart from general business advisory firms. The firm's dual offering through its affiliate creates a comprehensive service spectrum from preventative valuation and advisory work to crisis management and restructuring.

A key caveat is that Chartered Capital Advisers is not a business lender and does not provide loans or financing products. The company operates as a professional services firm offering advisory, analytical, and expert services primarily suited for mid-to-large corporate transactions and valuations. Consumers or small business owners seeking traditional business loans, lines of credit, or SBA financing should look elsewhere.
address: 245 Park Ave 39th Floor, New York, NY 10167

company_info.headquarters: 245 Park Ave 39th Floor, New York, NY 10167

pros: Team members have senior experience at Big Four accounting firms, investment banks, and commercial banks
Serves over 600 clients globally across diverse industries since 1988
Provides expert testimony services, indicating recognized credibility in legal proceedings
Affiliate company R. G. Quintero & Co. offers specialized turnaround and bankruptcy restructuring services
Offers fairness opinions, valuable for M&A transactions and shareholder disputes
Comprehensive valuation expertise including intangible assets and securities pricing
Long-established track record with NYC office since 1988
cons: Not a lender—does not provide any financing, loans, or credit products
Services are oriented toward mid-to-large corporate clients and complex transactions, not small business owners
Website lacks pricing information, fee structures, or engagement cost ranges
No mention of turnaround timelines or service delivery expectations
Limited information about accessibility or availability to emerging businesses or startups
best_for: Corporate clients seeking business valuations for M&A transactions or litigation
Investors and financial institutions requiring fairness opinions or due diligence analysis
Companies in financial distress needing restructuring or bankruptcy advisory (via affiliate)
Law firms and accounting firms seeking expert valuation testimony and analysis
diagnosis: Chartered Capital Advisers is best suited for corporate clients, investors, and professional service providers (attorneys, accountants) needing specialized valuation and M&A advisory services—not for consumers or businesses seeking actual business loans or financing. The main caveat is that despite the 'business-loans' categorization, this firm provides no lending products whatsoever and focuses exclusively on high-level advisory and analytical services for complex corporate transactions.
services: Pricing of options, warrants, equity, and debt securities
Intangible asset and unique asset/liability valuation
Goodwill and intangible asset impairment analyses
Financial and operational review of acquisition candidates
Financial restructuring and turnarounds (via affiliate R. G. Quintero & Co.)
